content:
Hi folks, I have a few patches out to improve our rust-analyzer support. If folks find value in these tools I'd appreciate your reviews:

  • support for generated bindings out of tree: https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250210-rust-analyzer-bindings-include-v2-0-23dff845edc3@gmail.com/
  • support for std crates in the macros crate (minimal, intended for rust-fixes): https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250210-rust-analyzer-macros-core-dep-v3-1-45eb4836f218@gmail.com/
  • support for std crates in the macros crate (better, splits host and target crates, intended for rust-next): https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250209-rust-analyzer-host-v1-0-a2286a2a2fa3@gmail.com/

Thanks.

During my own development I noticed that the ffi crate is also not listed as a dependency of either bindings or uapi, even though its values are used in the generated bindings. It's not listed for the kernel crate either, which also uses and re-exports the types. Seems like it was forgotten when the ffi crate was introduced in d072acda4862f.

Including it would be similar to your fix to include the uapi crate, so very simple. I saw that I'm a bit late with this though, since your patch was already merged.

Should I send a small patch to include it? Would be nice for me to get familiar with the mailing list flow as I haven't used that yet.

Lukas Fischer said:

Should I send a small patch to include it? Would be nice for me to get familiar with the mailing list flow as I haven't used that yet.

yeah that's a good idea for a first patch :)

What should I base this on? master, rust-next, or maybe Tamir's current patch series for rust-analyzer (https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250325-rust-analyzer-host-v5-0-385e7f1e1e23@gmail.com/) directly?

normally one bases their work on rust-next or the latest tag from Linus' git (currently that is v6.14 IIRC). If your patch series depends on Tamir's patches, then of course you should put your patches on top
@Lukas Fischer I don't think you need to depend on my patches.

Sent: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250404125150.85783-2-kernel@o1oo11oo.de/

The commit message is very much inspired from yours here, not sure if that needs a tag as well.

The crates don't really have much of an order, so I just added it at the bottom of the ones without generated content.
No tag needed. Looks good to me.
although ... does it need compiler_builtins?
Good question, I just assumed it would otherwise run into problems if lang items or something important is not defined, but those probably already come from core.
The makefile does depend ffi on it, but i don't understand why. I think only core needs to depend on it, but currently only build_error does. I think this is all a hack...?
Since compiler_builtins only defines a bunch of intrinsics which ffi does not use, because ffi only defines type aliases, I guess it could do without?
But it should probably match the actual crate hierarchy defined in the Makefile
Yeah, I think your patch is correct. Whether the Makefile is, that's another story. I think it's written this way because there's no core target
Scratch that. There's core.o.

Maybe @Benno Lossin knows why? build_error, pin_init, ffi all depend on compiler_builtins.

https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/e48e99b6edf41c69c5528aa7ffb2daf3c59ee105/rust/Makefile#L500-L510

compiler_builtins depends on core: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/e48e99b6edf41c69c5528aa7ffb2daf3c59ee105/rust/Makefile#L494-L495

I wonder if any of this is necessary, maybe just the kernel crate should depend on compiler_builtins?
Maybe @Miguel Ojeda knows -- the first instance of this pattern was at the dawn of time: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blame/ef9e37973c3a50497c943e70d577dad10a8e41f2/rust/Makefile#L363-L373
Thanks for the review, I guess if there are no changes required I don't need to send a second revision just to include the Reviewed-by tag, do I?
No, you do not.

Tamir Duberstein said:

Maybe Benno Lossin knows why? build_error, pin_init, ffi all depend on compiler_builtins.

https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/e48e99b6edf41c69c5528aa7ffb2daf3c59ee105/rust/Makefile#L500-L510

compiler_builtins depends on core: https://github.com/torvalds/linux/blob/e48e99b6edf41c69c5528aa7ffb2daf3c59ee105/rust/Makefile#L494-L495

I wonder if any of this is necessary, maybe just the kernel crate should depend on compiler_builtins?

I got that patch mostly from Miguel Ojeda, so no idea :)

I got the message that the patch is missing a CC: stable tag, which in hindsight makes sense, since the commit it fixes has made it there. I read the stable process before submitting the patch, I just didn't think such a tooling fix would be important enough for a backport, since your change for the uapi crate also did not include the cc tag. Should I add that and resend (next week so that people had a few more working days to take a look) or ask Greg first if it should even go to stable?
I think you can just resend

The message is meant to get people used to not forget the Cc: stable tag when Fixes is used. At the same time, a very reasonable reader could definitely interpret the stable kernel rules as "this is not important enough". In practice, even trivial fixes are backported sometimes. The patch cannot really break anything important anyway, so it should be fine.

Since you got the message, I would just suggest resending. In other cases, maintainers may add the flags for you anyway when they take the patch, e.g. when I start picking fixes next week.

Thanks!

It's an easy change, so I can also just resend it now if no other comments are likely to come up. Backporting it will create conflicts since I based it on rust-next and pin_init is not in stable yet, but that is easy to resolve. Should I also include --cc 'stable@vger.kernel.org then? Or wait till next week?
Ah I see it picks it up from the Cc tag anyway.

Backporting it will create conflicts since I based it on rust-next and pin_init is not in stable yet, but that is easy to resolve.

Yeah, that is fine, no worries -- conflicts on backports are solved later, the patch needs to land in mainline first.

Or wait till next week?

Since it is a fix, I would resend it in a couple days. Since it is essentially the same patch, the review time counts for both, so there is no less there, no worries.

Following up on the above discussion regarding compiler_builtins, I sent https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250408-rust-remove-compiler-builtins-deps-v1-1-4fda4f187190@gmail.com/.

Though I just realized I need to rebase on rust-fixes to pick up @Lukas Fischer's patch.

I see it's already merged now, I wanted to send v2 earlier today but then I had some other things come up and I didn't check yesterday. I didn't change anything but add the Cc and Reviewed-by tags anyway.
in that case one normally doesn't send another version, the maintainer(s) will add the tags for you
